title: Prompt tile: shortcut digit and ? glyph vertically misaligned (Terminus)
description: draw_glyph_centered centered the ? using Pango pixel_extents, which report requested-size metrics for BITMAP fonts (Terminus), not the snapped/rendered glyph — so the ? sat off from the digit, which centers via the true scanned ink (run_ink). Fix: center the ? on text::ink_extent (the scanned ink), same metric as the digit.
close_reason: draw_glyph_centered now centers on text::ink_extent (true rendered ink) instead of Pango pixel_extents — matches the digit's metric. Measuring to confirm.

title: Tiles redraw at 60Hz (compositor rate) — high CPU/GPU, laptop runs hot
description: The frame-clock tick calls queue_draw every tick (~60Hz) with no rate limit, and each content tile does a per-frame femtovg GPU render+readback for a STATIC background. With several animated tiles this cooks the CPU/GPU. Fix: (1) throttle redraw to config.fps (cap), default ~20fps for auto-animated tiles; (2) skip the per-frame femtovg capture for content tiles without a shader — fill the background via Cairo instead (it's static). Idle/empty/static tiles ideally shouldn't animate at all (follow-up).
close_reason: Throttle frame-clock redraw to target fps (DEFAULT_ANIM_FPS=20, or config.fps) instead of 60Hz; skip per-frame femtovg GPU render+readback for content tiles w/o shader (static bg filled in Cairo). Big CPU/GPU reduction.

title: <icon> inline embed (flow integration, color tint)
description: Add 'icon' to EMBED_TAGS. <icon name="git"/> or <icon src="/path.svg"/>, sized to the neighbour digit ink box + centered on the mid-line (reuse the alignment). Optional color attr tints the SVG (silhouette via alpha mask) for monochrome UI icons; without color, render full-colour (app logos).
close_reason: <icon name|src color> inline embed; sized to neighbour digit ink (skips empty-ink runs, falls back to line cap), centered on mid-line. Gallery of 8 tinted icons + digit alignment vision-verified.

title: Bundled icon registry + starter SVG set
description: icons/ dir embedded via include_str! (registry like src/tiles). Author a clean starter set (e.g. git/folder/terminal/check/bell/arrow-up/code/browser/generic-app). Lookup by name; external file via src=.
close_reason: icons/ dir + svg::BUNDLED registry (include_str!): folder/check/arrow-up/bell/code/terminal/gear/app. Lookup by name; src= for files.

title: SVG icons: bundled set + <icon> embed + app-icon variant
description: Drop reliance on icon fonts for arbitrary/app icons. Rasterize bundled (and path) SVGs via resvg (pure Rust) into RGBA and composite onto Cairo through the existing buffer path — pixel-perfect, unlimited glyphs. Add a composable <icon name|src> inline embed sized/aligned to the neighbour digit (keeps the big aligned aesthetic). Wire the deferred non-claude desktop branch to show the leftmost window's app icon. NOT redoing the Cairo status primitives (kept). Status colors already punched up.
close_reason: SVG icons done: resvg plumbing+cache, bundled set, <icon> embed, app-icon variant, docs. NOT redoing Cairo status (per scope). 42 tests, clippy/fmt clean, check green.

title: SVG rasterization → Cairo composite (resvg + tiny-skia) with cache
description: Add resvg dep. rasterize_svg(bytes, px) -> premultiplied RGBA via resvg/tiny-skia (fit + center). Composite onto Cairo (premultiplied RGBA→ARGB32 BGRA, scaled by device scale, like the glow path). thread_local cache keyed by (icon-key, px) so animated tiles don't re-rasterize each frame. Thread device scale into draw_flow.
close_reason: src/svg.rs rasterize_argb32 (resvg/tiny-skia → premultiplied BGRA, fit+center, optional tint mask); paint_argb32_at composite; thread_local ICON_CACHE keyed by (source,px,tint); device scale threaded into draw_flow. Builds, no warnings.

title: Line-1 baseline misalignment: number, symbol, and folder not aligned
description: In draw_flow each text run and the status symbol were vertically centered INDEPENDENTLY within line_h, so the large shortcut number, the base-size folder text, and the dot/?/cells did not share a baseline and the symbol wasn't on the number's optical center. Fix: two-pass per line — lay out runs, take max ascent/descent → shared baseline_y (text block centered in the line); paint each run on baseline_y - its_baseline; center inline symbols on the text optical centre (baseline_y - 0.36*ascent). Vision-verified all 4 states + the single-line compose path.
notes: Superseded the optical-center heuristic with ink-box matching: each inline symbol is sized to (and vertically aligned with) the neighbouring digit's measured Pango ink rect, so dot/cells/? span exactly the digit's top→baseline. Verified at 3x zoom + normal size.
close_reason: Shared-baseline two-pass layout in draw_flow; symbols centered on text optical centre. Vision-checked working/prompt/idle/shell + regression on the icon+ticker+value line. 42 tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Visible yellow glow behind the prompt ? (Cairo radial bloom)
description: The prompt ? should have a real, VISIBLE yellow glow/bloom — not a few px. Draw a Cairo radial-gradient halo behind the ? in draw_status (inside the pulse group so it pulses too).
close_reason: glow_halo(): Cairo radial bloom behind the prompt ? (radius 0.95×slot, yellow #f9e2af, inside the pulse group). Vision-verified visible.

title: claude tile — demo feedback round 2 (glow, idle-ago, bigger symbols, commit-in-marquee)
description: Second live-demo feedback on the bundled claude tile.
close_reason: All 4 round-2 items done + vision-verified; 42 tests, clippy/fmt clean. Re-demoed.

title: Prompt is the attention state: whole-tile pulse + brighter yellow
description: The prompt (yellow ?) state is the one to be drawn to. Make the WHOLE tile pulse when state=prompt — shortcut number AND window title blink together, not just the ?. Implement a composable <pulse>…</pulse> wrapper (a Processed.pulse flag) that the renderer animates via Cairo push_group + paint_with_alpha(osc); the claude template wraps its content in <pulse> only when state=='prompt'. Brighten the yellow (#ebcb8b→#f9e2af) and make the ? bold/static-bright (the tile pulse provides the blink, avoiding double-oscillation). Pulse floor kept high (~0.4) so it never disappears.
close_reason: Composable <pulse> wrapper (markup PULSE_TAG + Processed.pulse) → whole-tile push_group+paint_with_alpha(osc, floor 0.40). claude template wraps content in <pulse> when state=='prompt'; ? now bright static #f9e2af (no double-osc). Verified: number+folder+title+? all dim together across bright/dim frames.

title: Marquee: render static when content fits; slower default speed
description: The marquee scrolls (and loops with the ◆) even for short titles that would fit the box — pointless and nauseating. In draw_ticker, if the inner text width fits within the box, render it statically (left-aligned, no loop unit, no ◆). Also reduce TICKER_SPEED (120→~70 logical px/s) — it's too fast for comfortable reading.
close_reason: draw_ticker renders static (left-aligned, no ◆) when text fits the box; TICKER_SPEED 120→70. Verified short title identical across frames.

title: claude tile — demo feedback round 1 (legibility, prompt attention, marquee, width)
description: First live-demo feedback on the bundled claude tile. Visuals read too undertoned; the prompt state must grab attention; the marquee needs tuning; the tile is too wide.
close_reason: All 5 feedback items done + vision-verified; 42 tests, clippy/fmt clean. Re-demoed.

title: Brighten undertoned tile elements (status indicators, ↑commit badge, shortcut)
description: On the live bar the status indicators and the ↑N commit badge read too dim/undertoned, making the tile hard to read at a glance. Raise the indicator blink/pulse alpha floors (working 0.18→~0.45, shell 0.40→~0.55) and slightly enlarge the dot; make the ↑N badge bold and brighter; ensure the shortcut number is bold/legible. Keep the brand colours (#d97757/#5ccfe6) but never let them fade so far they vanish.
close_reason: Raised indicator alpha floors (working 0.18→0.45, shell 0.40→0.55), dot radius 0.26→0.30; ↑N badge bold; title brightened #a6adc8→#cdd6f4. Vision-verified.

title: claude tile preset + JSON Schema data contract + mocked samples + docs
description: Author the bundled tile under tiles/claude/: tile.json (geometry 300x50, font, colors, the multi-line minijinja format template binding {{ shortcut }}/{{ state }}/{{ idle_level }}/{{ folder }}/{{ title }}/{{ unpushed }}); schema.json (draft-07 JSON Schema documenting the state enum, idle level, and MOCK-vs-REAL per field); samples/{working,prompt,idle,shell}.json (varied: long title, unpushed count, idle level); README.md (binding-contract prose + how to use tile/tile_file + pwetty CLI). Update main README.md with a 'Bundled tiles & the data contract' section. Vision-verify the rendered tile at 300x50 across all states + a long-title marquee at two timestamps.
close_reason: Bundled claude tile authored: tiles/claude/{tile.json (300x50, multi-line template),schema.json (draft-07, mock-vs-real annotated),samples/{working,prompt,idle,shell}.json,README.md}. Main README section added. Vision-verified all 4 states via the real preset path (pwetty render): correct shortcuts, distinct indicators, ↑N badge hidden when 0, looping marquee.

title: Animated <status> inline element (working/prompt/idle/shell)
description: Add 'status' to EMBED_TAGS. draw_status(cr, attrs, rect, time) reading state(+level): working->filled circle #d97757 alpha-blink ~1.1s; prompt-> '?' glyph (small centered layout_line) #ebcb8b alpha-blink ~1.0s; shell->filled circle #5ccfe6 opacity pulse 1.0<->0.4 ~1.6s; idle->two shaded cells colored by level 0-6 (#ffffff..#3a3a3a) static. Colors via render::parse_hex_color; blink(time,period) helper. Broaden the frame-clock trigger to has_anim = format contains '<tickerbox' || '<status' (lib.rs:204-215). Unit-test status attr parsing. File: src/lib.rs.
close_reason: Added <status> inline element + draw_status (working orange-blink / shell cyan-pulse / prompt yellow-? blink / idle 7-level fade cells). Broadened frame-clock trigger to <status. Vision-verified all 4 states distinct + blink across frames.

title: Multi-line inline flow composition (generalize draw_inline -> draw_flow)
description: draw_inline is single-line only; a tall/multi-line tile needs flow. processed.markup keeps literal \n and embed placeholders in document order, and processed.embeds is in that same order. draw_flow: split markup on \n into lines; per line split on EMBED_PLACEHOLDER; consume embeds via a front-cursor; stack lines vertically (line_h=font_size*1.4, block vertically centered); paint text segments via text::layout_line advancing x, draw embeds in gaps. Left pad -> font-based (font_size*0.5), re-verify existing single-line ticker demo unaffected. Embed width by tag: tickerbox->embed_width, status->line_h. draw_content calls draw_flow whenever embeds present. Unit-test the line/segment/embed-assignment splitter. File: src/lib.rs.
close_reason: Generalized draw_inline->draw_flow: multi-line composition via pure flow_layout() splitter (text runs + embed refs in document order). 5 new unit tests. Vision-verified 2-line claude tile at 300x50; single-line ticker demo no-regression.

title: Tile-preset packaging: embedded presets + file override + JSON deep-merge
description: Switch the CFFI Module::Config assoc type to serde_json::Value (waybar-cffi only needs DeserializeOwned). In init: read optional 'tile' (embedded preset name) and/or 'tile_file' (path), load the preset JSON, deep-merge with preset as base and the waybar config winning on any set key (new pure recursive merge(base,over) helper, unit-tested), then serde_json::from_value into the existing typed Config (extra keys ignored). New src/tiles/mod.rs: TilePreset { name, config, schema, doc, samples } via include_str!; get(name)/all(). Preset config is plain JSON (no comment stripper, zero new deps). Files: src/lib.rs, src/tiles/mod.rs.
close_reason: type Config=serde_json::Value; config::resolve layers bundled/file preset under module keys via recursive merge(). src/tiles registry (include_str! presets). 7 new tests (merge/resolve/registry). Build+42 tests green.

title: Bundled 'claude desktop' tile: packaging + multi-line render + introspection
description: The claude tile is why pwetty exists. Ship a bundled, beautifully-documented tile that renders a niri desktop's claude session: shortcut number + animated status indicator + folder + window-title marquee + unpushed-commits badge. Data is FULLY MOCKED here; a downstream agent supplies real data. Three pillars: (1) package the tile config WITH the module (embedded preset + file override, JSON deep-merge), (2) render it (multi-line inline flow + animated <status> element), (3) document it (JSON Schema data contract + 'pwetty' CLI). Faithful vocabulary from claude-status-db (not a dependency): states working/prompt/idle/shell; real fields cwd/window_id/state; title/unpushed/shortcut/app_icon mocked. Default form factor 300x50, two-line. Plan: /home/zor/.claude/plans/enumerated-soaring-cookie.md
close_reason: All 5 children done: packaging(3te), multi-line flow(x2f), <status>(dha), claude tile+schema+docs(0ne), pwetty CLI(gyt). Bundled claude tile renders 4 states; data decoupled via JSON Schema + pwetty check/schema/render. 42 tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: claude2 preset: two Claude sessions on one desktop
description: A new bundled preset rendering two Claude sessions stacked on one niri desktop tile. Shared shortcut in a left gutter spanning both rows; each session shows status + folder + unpushed badge + its window title. Data via a sessions[] array. Targets a ~78px bar.

title: <bg preset> masked translucent shader backgrounds
description: New markup tag <bg preset='name' k='v'/> selecting a named, bundled shader preset as a MILD background layer: composited at moderate alpha (~0.28), masked to the focus-bubble rounded-rect (mirrors draw_active_panel) with a sharp 20px inward fade. Generic mask injected in a new wrap_fragment_masked; preset registry in shader.rs (night, caustic); rendered in draw_content via ShaderCache::render_masked + paint_rgba_at, behind the active card + content. Gating: needs_gl + could_anim + content_animates recognise <bg. night.glsl tweaked: brighter/bigger/slower stars.
close_reason: Implemented + gated (48 tests, clippy, fmt).

title: Active-card padding + idle 'ago' as K-2000 arc badge
description: (1) active card crowded text -> bumped global content padding (alignment preserved). (2) inline 'Nm ago' overflowed neighbor tiles -> moved into idle badge: time on an arc circling the two bars, mild STATIC K-2000 red. Template passes idle_ago into <status ago=...>; box widens for idle-with-ago.
close_reason: Done; gates green (45 tests, clippy, pwetty check).

title: Fix claude-tile doc drift: marquee→wrap, dot→Claude mascot
description: The data-contract docs described the pre-pivot design. Fixed: README.md, tiles/claude/README.md, tiles/claude/schema.json now say the title is word-wrapped (not a marquee), the indicator is the pixel-art Claude mascot (working=orange, shell=cyan, +glow) with prompt=? whole-tile pulse, and unpushed shows after the folder (idle shows idle_ago). Also documented <wrap> and <sep> embeds. Field contract unchanged, so existing consumer JSON stays valid.
close_reason: Done this session; gates green (43 tests, clippy, pwetty check claude).

title: Streaming/push exec for sub-second tile refresh (cffi consumer)
description:
Filed by the claude-status consumer (~/Perso/claude-status-db). CONTEXT: claude-status drives a row of per-desktop cffi/pwetty tiles via exec; it needs the active-desktop highlight to update within a few hundred ms on a niri desktop switch. The consumer-side exec is already ~3ms (a cache read), so the only remaining latency is pwetty refresh cadence.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR (src/content.rs from_config): a thread loops run_command(exec) via std .output (which blocks until the process EXITS and captures all stdout) then sleeps interval WHOLE seconds (Duration from_secs; interval 0 = run once). So refresh is poll-only with a 1s floor and no push path; the active tile lags up to ~1s on switch.

ASK: a streaming/push exec mode. Spawn the command ONCE and read stdout line-by-line via BufReader lines(), calling ContentStore::set per line, instead of .output once per interval. The existing 150ms redraw timer + dirty flag (src/lib.rs near line 264) then repaints within ~150ms of each pushed line. One long-lived process per tile: no respawn churn, no polling. Suggest gating with a stream:true flag (default false) so the current interval poll path is byte-for-byte unchanged; or treat interval 0 plus a continuously-emitting command as streaming.

PRODUCER CONTRACT: the command stays alive and prints one newline-delimited JSON object whenever data changes; pwetty applies each line as new content (same parse_data). On EOF/exit keep last content (optionally restart with backoff); skip malformed lines.

ACCEPTANCE: a streaming-command tile repaints within ~150ms of a new stdout line; existing interval tiles unaffected; a streaming process that exits does not busy-loop.

ALTERNATIVES (less preferred): fractional/millisecond interval; or honor the waybar signal refresh.

CONSUMER READINESS: claude-status will ship a tile-watch producer (emits a fresh line on every desktop change via an event-updated cache + inotify) the moment this lands. Consumer-side tracking: claude-status-db-9h4.
close_reason: Implemented stream:true push mode in content.rs (spawn-once + BufReader lines + set per line; EOF keeps last content, respawns after 1s backoff; blank lines skipped). Unconditional 150ms dirty poll repaints; no lib.rs change. Docs + 2 tests added. Gates green (45 tests, clippy, pwetty check).

title: Claude robot-face builtin: prominence + use for prompt state
description: The claude-face SVG is bundled and used for working (orange) / shell (cyan), but: (a) at tile scale it's small/easy to miss, and (b) the prompt state still uses a plain ? glyph rather than the face. Consider making the face the consistent indicator (e.g. prompt = the face with an attention treatment) and/or sizing it up. Needs a design decision.
notes: The bundled claude-face.svg was a generic cartoon robot head, not the Claude mark. Replaced this session with the actual pixel-art Claude mascot (rounded orange body, 2 square eyes punched as evenodd holes so tint keeps them readable, side nubs, 3 legs) — matches the #d97757 sticker. Used for working (orange) + shell (cyan) with color-matched glow. Prompt stays a yellow '?' per user preference ('the thing I need to be drawn to'). Remaining open question is ONLY mascot prominence/sizing at tile scale.

title: Status indicator: bundled claude robot-face SVG (working/shell) instead of a plain dot
description: Replaced the working (orange) and shell (cyan) filled circles with a bundled, original 'claude-face' robot-face SVG, tinted to the state colour, composited at the blink/pulse alpha, with the color-matched glow. Added icons/claude-face.svg to the bundled set (also usable as <icon name='claude-face'>); raster_svg_cached + paint alpha; threaded scale into draw_status. prompt(?)/idle(bar)/empty(ring) unchanged.
close_reason: claude-face bundled SVG; working=orange face, shell=cyan face, tinted+blinking+glow. Verified.

title: Window tile: shortcut + app name baseline-aligned, not v-centered
description: In the window line, the large shortcut and base-size app name shared one Pango run, so Pango baseline-aligned them (app sat low) instead of vertical-centering. Added a thin <sep/> embed (zero content, ~0.4cap gap) that splits the run so each span gets independent ink-centering. Window template uses <sep/> between shortcut and app.
close_reason: <sep/> run-splitter; shortcut/app now independently ink-centered. Verified at 3x.

title: Window tile: hero-icon left gutter (big icon + marquee below, 2-line)
description: Single-line window layout was inconsistent with the 2-line claude tiles. A leading <icon hero='1'/> now draws as a big left gutter spanning the whole tile height (vertically centered); the text lines (shortcut+app on line1, title marquee on line2) indent past it. Matches the claude 2-line structure while keeping a prominent crisp app icon.
close_reason: draw_flow detects a leading <icon hero>, draws it as a full-height left gutter, indents lines past it. Window template leads with hero icon; shortcut+app line1, title marquee line2. Verified firefox/kitty; claude tiles unchanged.

title: XDG icon-theme resolver: <icon name> falls back to system icon lookup
description: The main non-claude seam: turning a niri window app_id into an icon. Today the data agent must resolve a full .svg path. Make <icon name="firefox"> (and app_icon="firefox") fall back to searching the system icon theme (/usr/share/icons, ~/.local/share/icons, ~/.icons; honor theme + size dirs; .svg preferred, .png fallback) when the name isn't bundled. Then the agent passes the niri app_id directly and pwetty finds the logo. Keep bundled names + explicit paths working. Cache resolutions.

title: High-def app icons: <icon size> + icon-forward window layout
description: App icons rendered ~18px (sized to the digit cap) — too small/low-def for a logo. Added <icon size=N> multiplier; redesigned the window (is_claude=false) layout to a single icon-forward line: shortcut + a big (1.9x) crisp app icon + app/title marquee. Real freedesktop logos (firefox/kitty via path) now render large + crisp (~38px, fox region 10px→21px). claude layout unchanged.
close_reason: icon_size attr + icon-forward single-line window layout; verified firefox/kitty render big+crisp.

title: Harden the data contract for the downstream agent (schema required if/then + render --data)
description: Audit found: schema 'required' wrongly demanded state+folder for the window (is_claude=false) layout (the bundled window sample violated it); and there was no way for the data agent to validate THEIR json. Fix: required=[shortcut] + allOf if/then/else (window→app_icon/app, claude→state); add 'pwetty render <tile> --data <file|->' to render the agent's own JSON; docs.
close_reason: schema required=[shortcut] + allOf if/then/else (window→app_icon/app, claude→state) — fixes the window sample violating its own required; added 'pwetty render --data <file|->' so the data agent renders its OWN json (verified with a real freedesktop firefox.svg); README + contract docs updated.

title: Differentiate the active (focused) desktop tile
description: The claude tiles are really niri DESKTOP tiles; nothing currently shows which desktop is the active/focused one. Add an 'active' (bool) data field + a visual treatment applied when active — candidate treatments: a subtle accent background panel behind the tile, a left/bottom accent stripe, or a border/brightened content. Needs: schema field 'active', a renderer mechanism (e.g. a Processed.active flag set by an <active/> marker the template emits when active, drawn as a rounded accent in draw_content before the flow), and an updated sample. Treatment choice TBD; iterate via demo.
close_reason: Active-desktop differentiation: <active/> marker → Processed.active → draw_active_panel (accent fill+border 'card') behind the tile (steady, outside the pulse group). 'active' field in claude+empty schemas; working sample active=true. Vision-verified (border on active, none on inactive).

title: Compact tile for empty desktops (digit + empty symbol, stacked)
description: An empty desktop (no windows — e.g. the user's desktop 7) doesn't warrant a full-width 240px tile. Render a narrow, compact variant: just the shortcut digit + an 'empty' symbol, vertically stacked and centered. Needs: an 'is_empty' (bool) data field + a template branch; an 'empty' indicator (e.g. a dim hollow ring as a new <status state='empty'/> or a bundled icon); the empty desktop's waybar module uses a narrow width (e.g. ~56px). Likely also center-alignment support in the flow for the compact layout (currently left-aligned). Add an empty sample.
close_reason: Compact 'empty' bundled preset (56px, align=center): shortcut stacked over a dim hollow <status state=empty> ring. Added config.align + center support in draw_flow (per-line total advance). Fixed a cross-cutting Cairo bug: arc helpers (dot/ring/glow) now new_sub_path() so no stray connector line. Vision-verified.

title: Electric cyan + color-matched dot glow (bursting-bubble)
description: shell cyan #00d3ee still read baby-blue; pushed to electric #12f5ff (high G+B, low R). Added a moderate color-matched glow_halo under both working/shell dots, alpha riding the same osc as the dot (bursting-bubble activity feel).
close_reason: shell→#12f5ff electric; moderate color-matched glow under working+shell dots, pulsing with the dot. Verified.

title: Slow pulses a touch more + raise dot color saturation
description: Pulsing a tad fast still; bump periods (working 1.7→2.0, shell 2.2→2.8, prompt pulse 1.0→1.3). Increase saturation of the dot colors: orange #ff6a2c→#ff5a14, cyan #15d1e6→#00d3ee.
close_reason: working 1.7→2.0s, shell 2.2→2.8s, prompt pulse 1.0→1.3s; orange #ff6a2c→#ff5a14, cyan #15d1e6→#00d3ee. Built, gates green.

title: Blink dots: slower + wider amplitude (deeper fade)
description: working/shell dots blink too fast with too-narrow amplitude (floors 0.45/0.55). Slow the cycle and deepen the fade to match the daemon's feel (working ~18%). working: period 1.1→1.7, floor 0.45→0.15; shell pulse: period 1.6→2.2, floor 0.55→0.25.
close_reason: working 1.1s/0.45→1.7s/0.15, shell 1.6s/0.55→2.2s/0.25 — slower, deep fade. Verified bright vs dim frames.

title: App-icon variant for non-claude desktops
description: Wire the deferred branch: {% if is_claude %}<status .../>{% else %}<icon name='{{ app }}'/>{% endif %} in the claude tile. Add is_claude (bool) + app (string) to schema + a non-claude sample. Document mock-vs-real.
close_reason: claude tile {% if is_claude %} branch: non-claude shows shortcut + app icon (<icon src/name>, path-or-bundled via '/' in app_icon) + app label + title marquee. Added is_claude/app/app_icon to schema + window.json sample. check green; window tile vision-verified.

title: Document + illustrate the bundled icon set in the docs
description: USER-REQUESTED. Document the bundled SVG icon set (names + previews) in README/online docs, alongside the tile/schema docs, so the downstream agent knows what's available and how to reference it (<icon name>/src). Include a rendered illustration/gallery.
close_reason: README 'Icons (bundled SVGs)' section (<icon name/src/color>, bundled set list + docs/icons.png gallery); improved gear icon to a real cog; tiles/claude/README contract updated with idle_ago/is_claude/app/app_icon + the two-layout explanation.

title: Punch up status indicator colors (orange + cyan too desaturated)
description: The working orange (#d97757) reads muddy and the shell cyan (#5ccfe6) reads baby-blue — low saturation, worsened by the blink alpha floor. Bump both to punchier, higher-saturation hues while keeping the hue family.
close_reason: working #d97757→#ff6a2c, shell #5ccfe6→#15d1e6 — saturated/punchy. Vision-verified.

title: Configurable font family; bundled claude tile prefers Terminus
description: Added Config.font_family (Pango family or comma list; default 'sans'), threaded through all tile text + the status ? glyph. The bundled claude tile sets font_family='Terminus' (installed as bitmap .otb, family 'Terminus'). pwetty check stays green; vision-verified the Terminus pixel look.
close_reason: font_family config option added + threaded; claude preset uses Terminus. Verified rendering.

title: Bigger shortcut digit + matching symbol (xx-large), vertical-center text
description: Make the line-1 digit (and the auto-matched status symbol) bigger; keep folder/marquee text size. Switched text positioning from shared-baseline to ink-center vertical centering (smaller text centers against the big number instead of baseline-locking). Number span large→xx-large; dot bumped to 0.58·cap for optical parity (circles read smaller than box-filling glyphs).
close_reason: Number xx-large; symbol follows via ink-box match; text ink-centered (not baseline). Dot optical bump 0.5→0.58·cap. Vision-verified at 3x + normal.

title: Nest the ↑commit count inside the marquee (line 2)
description: Move the ↑N commit badge off line 1 and into the tickerbox on line 2, prefixing the title — declutters line 1 for the bigger symbol+number+folder.
close_reason: ↑N moved off line 1 into the line-2 tickerbox, prefixing the title (bold green). Line 1 declutters for the bigger symbol+number+folder. Verified.

title: Match status symbol size to the shortcut number; make both bigger
description: The status indicator (dot/?) should visually match the shortcut number's size, and both should be bigger. Bump preset font_size 13→15, keep shortcut bold/large, and size the dot radius + ? glyph to match the number.
close_reason: font_size 13→15; dot radius →0.42, idle cell height →0.62, ? glyph →0.92×slot — symbols now read at the number's scale, all bigger. Verified.

title: Idle tiles show a relative 'Xm ago' / 'Yh ago'
description: Paused (idle) sessions should display how long since active, e.g. '12m ago'. Add an idle_ago string field (REAL-derived from last_talk_ts; agent supplies the formatted string) to the schema + samples, and render it on line 1 for state=idle.
close_reason: Added idle_ago (string) to schema+samples; template renders '{{ idle_ago }} ago' (small grey) on line 1 when state=idle. Verified '12m ago'.

title: Narrow the claude tile ~20% (300→240) and refit the marquee width
description: Tile is a touch too wide. Drop the preset width 300→240 and reduce the line-2 tickerbox width (270→~210) to fit with margins. Re-vision-check the 2-line layout at 240×50.
close_reason: Preset width 300→240, tickerbox 270→210. Re-vision-checked 2-line layout at 240×50.

title: Marquee: let the ◆ clear the line before restarting (non-seamless loop)
description: Currently the loop tiles copies seamlessly so a new copy enters as the old leaves — busy/nauseating. Instead, after the text + ◆ scroll off, let the box go fully BLANK for a beat before the next copy enters. Implement by making the loop period = content width + a clearing gap >= box width, so there is an interval where the box shows nothing.
close_reason: Loop period = unit width + 1.25× box width, so the box fully clears between passes (◆ exits, blank beat, then restart).

title: pwetty introspection CLI (check / schema / render / list)
description: New src/bin/pwetty.rs + Cargo [[bin]] (crate already has rlib). Subcommands: list (bundled tiles); schema <tile> (print schema.json, the downstream contract); check [tile] (parse+merge preset, collect minijinja Template::undeclared_variables, render template against every bundled sample (must succeed), cross-check template vars vs schema properties warning on drift, print contract + per-sample OK/err, nonzero exit on failure); render <tile> [--sample|--all-states] [--time] [--out] (PNG for vision checks + downstream agent). Factor the offscreen render into pub fn render_png(config, markup, time, out) in the lib, shared by the bin AND examples/render_content.rs. Files: src/bin/pwetty.rs, Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, examples/render_content.rs.
close_reason: src/bin/pwetty.rs: list/schema/check/render. check cross-checks template undeclared_variables vs schema properties + renders every sample. render uses new pub render_png (shared with examples). cairo png feature promoted to normal dep. Verified: list/check(exit0)/render --all-states all work.

title: Make tickerbox a composable inline embed
description: Reworked tickerbox from a whole-tile takeover into an inline embed placed in the text flow: <tickerbox width=N>...</tickerbox> reserves a width-px box that surrounding text composes around. markup gains an Embed type + EMBED_PLACEHOLDER (object-replacement char) + process(effect_tags, embed_tags) pulling the inner markup out. draw_inline composes a single line of text segments interleaved with embed boxes (each text run laid out via text::layout_line and advanced past; the ticker drawn into its measured box). Tried Pango shape attributes first but gtk-rs AttrShape reservation didn't take, so used reliable manual segment layout. Verified headless: 'NOW [scrolling ticker] 12:00' composed on one line, scroll/clip confirmed.
close_reason: Inline-composable ticker; verified headless (label + ticker box + value on one line). 27 tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Scrolling <tickerbox> marquee
description: <tickerbox>TEXT</tickerbox> renders content wider than the tile as a single-line horizontal marquee: clipped to the tile, scrolling right-to-left at ~120 px/s, looping continuously with a diamond marker at the loop seam. markup::extract_tickerbox (pure, tested) pulls the inner markup; draw_content early-returns to draw_ticker which lays it out single-line (text::layout_line, no wrap), clips, and tiles copies of (text + marker) across the viewport at a time-driven offset. Animation auto-enabled when the format contains a tickerbox. Verified headless (frames at t=0/5/8 showing scroll + seamless loop + marker).
close_reason: Implemented + vision-verified headless (scroll, clip, seamless loop, marker). 27 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Fix glow GL-state leak; faithful headless harness
description: Span-glow rendered glitchy in the live widget (flickering white) because femtovg's background capture left GL_BLEND on with garbage in the FBO, and the glow shader pass blended over it. Fix: ShaderPass::render now disables blending and clears the FBO before drawing (deterministic state). The offscreen harness missed it because render_content didn't run femtovg before the glow; made it faithful (runs the femtovg bg layer first, like the live path) so this class of GL-state bug reproduces headless. Exposed composite_rgba. Recorded the verify-headless-first + demo-at-bottom habits in AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Fixed + verified via the now-faithful headless harness (femtovg+glow renders clean).

title: Span-level glow effect + shader cache
description: Span-level GPU shader effects: a ShaderCache (compile-once by key) plus a built-in glow shader. The <glow color=> custom tag renders a soft pulsing GPU halo behind a text span, positioned via the Pango layout (markup::process -> span_rect) and composited behind the text. Establishes the span-shader seam (cache threaded into draw_content via EffectCtx) that user per-span <shader src> will reuse. paint_rgba_at composites at a span offset; EFFECT_TAGS gains glow; lib.rs merges the two draw layers under one GL context. Validated offscreen and live waybar (conditional glow via {% if %}).
close_reason: Implemented + vision-validated (offscreen + live waybar conditional glow). 26 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Data-reactive shader uniforms
description: shader_uniforms config maps float uniform names to templates evaluated against the tile data (true/false to 1/0, else parsed as float); resolved each refresh and fed to the background shader, so the GPU shader reacts to live data. content.rs build_uniforms (pure, unit-tested) stored in TileContent; shader.rs ShaderPass::render sets named float uniforms; lib.rs passes the content uniforms through. examples/shaders/reactive.glsl + render_shader name=value args. Validated offscreen (calm teal at u_load=0.08 vs intense red at 0.95) and live waybar (load value drives the shader red).
close_reason: Implemented + vision-validated (offscreen low/high + live waybar). 26 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Tile-level GPU background shaders (Shadertoy-style)
description: background_shader config points at a Shadertoy-style GLSL fragment shader (defines mainImage) rendered on our own GL context into a texture with iResolution/iTime/iFrame, read back, and composited as the tile background; Pango content draws on top. Hot-reloaded on file mtime change; compile errors logged. Animated when fps>0 or a shader is set. New src/shader.rs (glow), examples/render_shader + examples/shaders/aurora.glsl. Data-driven uniforms are the fast-follow. Validated offscreen + live waybar (animated shader behind a data-bound clock).
close_reason: Implemented + vision-validated (offscreen frame + live waybar). 25 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Data-bound tile templates (minijinja + JSON data)
description: Tile content is now a data-bound template: exec emits JSON (the data model), and  is a minijinja template (Jinja {{ }}/{% %}) rendered against it into Pango markup, with HTML/XML autoescape so bound values are markup-safe. Object fields bind at top level ({{ cpu.pct }}); non-object data is {{ value }}. Filters + conditionals enable in-template formatting/state styling; data can also carry colors. Replaces the {} substituter and the deferred jq in one crate. markup::render_template (pure, unit-tested); content.rs parse_data+build_markup; examples/render_data vision harness. Validated in real waybar (JSON clock tile).
close_reason: Implemented + vision-validated (offscreen dashboard + live waybar). 24 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Rich content: Pango markup + extensible custom effect tags
description: Tile content is now Pango markup (per-span color/size/weight/font, multiline) rendered via PangoCairo over the femtovg GPU layer. Custom effect tags routed away from Pango (markup::process, pure+unit-tested) and drawn by our renderer positioned via Pango span extents; <box> highlight implemented as the proof effect. Orchestrated via 2 subagents (markup router; Pango text renderer) + lead integration. Vision-tested offscreen (render_text, render_content) + live waybar.
close_reason: Implemented + vision-validated in real waybar. 22 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean. Effect seam proven (<box>); shaders/glow are the documented next step.

title: Tile content/data model (static + exec sources)
description: Foundational feature: tiles get content from a static text or a command re-run on an interval (non-blocking background thread + thread-safe ContentStore + dirty-flag poll). format template, icon glyph, multiline. New src/content.rs; config fields text/exec/interval/icon/format; TextTile in tile.rs; render/lib wiring. Validated in real waybar (live clock tile, multiline, transparent on translucent bar).
close_reason: Implemented + validated in real waybar; 8 unit tests, clippy/fmt clean.

title: Headless screenshot harness for waybar module
description: Offscreen capture harness mirroring demo-fyre, adapted for a real waybar loading libpwetty_box.so. Stack: cage(headless, LIBSEAT_BACKEND=noop) -> niri(winit) -> waybar(cffi/pwetty) -> grim. Critical safety: LIBSEAT_BACKEND=noop prevents libseat from contacting logind, so it cannot steal the active seat (validated: cage-alone run left niri --session PID unchanged). Files under test/.
close_reason: Offscreen software render harness delivered: examples/render_tile.rs renders the tile via surfaceless EGL+femtovg to PNG (validated at t=0.6/2.0/7.5s, animated gradient + multiline text). Live-waybar harness defused (removed pkill niri) and marked DANGER.

title: Scaffold waybar CFFI shader/canvas widget (Rust cdylib)
description: Scaffold a Rust cdylib that loads as a waybar CFFI module and renders multiline text/icon tiles. Stack: waybar-cffi (GTK3), GtkGLArea + epoxy for the GL context, femtovg OpenGl renderer for canvas/text/icons. Constraint discovered: waybar v0.15 is GTK3 (GtkContainer* root), GL via GtkGLArea is the only zero-copy GPU surface; Vulkan not reachable in-widget. Scaffold must: build to .so, load in waybar, run an animated render loop on GtkGLArea via femtovg, plumb JSON config, expose a clean Tile abstraction seam, and ship a README with build + waybar config.

title: Prompt ?: tone down glow, saturate the glyph
description: Glow washed out the ? glyph. Glow alpha 0.85→0.55; glyph color #f9e2af→#ffd42a (saturated gold) for contrast.
close_reason: glow 0.85→0.55, glyph #f9e2af→#ffd42a. Verified.

title: Pause animation for tiles whose current content is static
description: Even after throttling, idle/empty/window/short-title tiles tick (cheap Cairo redraws) for no visual change. Detect when the current rendered content has no animated element (no working/prompt/shell status, no <pulse>, no overflowing ticker) and stop queuing draws until content changes (dirty). Would let the few genuinely-animating tiles run at a high fps essentially for free. (Ticker-overflow detection is data-dependent — treat tickerbox as potentially-animating, or check measured width vs box.)
close_reason: Implemented in a5a5fd9: ContentStore tracks animating; idle/empty/wrapped tiles don't repaint.

title: Increase the glow a bit on the working (orange) dot
description: Per live review: the orange working dot's color-matched glow could be a touch stronger. Bump its glow alpha multiplier (currently a*0.35 in draw_status) and/or radius (cap_h*0.95) a bit. Quick one-line tweak.
close_reason: working glow alpha a*0.35→a*0.5, radius cap_h*0.95→1.1. Verified.
